beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check weather and news.
beats.reasoning: Full energy start to Monday; Ethan wakes early to catch the sunny forecast and is aware of the 11am FIFA match. Morning routine is quick and purposeful.

beats.activity: Commute to Ballard office by bike and bus.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure (9am–3pm) between Westlake and U-District is downstream; Ethan takes an alternate route via bus/bike. Ballard office is direct from Eastlake via local streets.

beats.activity: Morning data analysis work: review weekend sales metrics and dashboard updates.
beats.reasoning: Settles into work routine early; anxious personality means he wants to be ahead before the mid-morning distraction of the FIFA match.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby bar for USA vs Wales match.
beats.reasoning: Spontaneous decision to catch the 11am FIFA match at a local bar; Ballard has strong soccer culture. Only 10 min walk from office.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales match, grab a burger and beer.
beats.reasoning: Ethan is spontaneous and takes advantage of the early-opening bars; the match is at 11am exactly, and he's there for it. Meal integrated into watch time.
Return from tavern after match; short walk to refocus on afternoon work.

beats.activity: Afternoon and early evening data work: compile weekly metrics and prepare reports.
beats.reasoning: High-achieving nature drives focus after lunch; anxiety about week ahead motivates solid afternoon output. Stays late to finish strong.

beats.activity: Commute home by bus and bike; avoids Light Rail closure still in effect (ends 3pm but he leaves after).
beats.reasoning: End of workday; takes same alternate route home via local transit. Gorgeous sunny evening means he'll likely spend time outdoors or on a hobby.

beats.activity: Grab dinner at favorite bagel spot, then browse vinyl at local record store.
beats.reasoning: Spontaneous decision to return to Ballard for his favorite bagel (strong opinions on bagels) and vinyl hunting. Sunny weather and good mood from match win make him want to stay out.
Final return to Eastlake after evening leisure; plenty of time before bedtime.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather—one of those rare June mornings that makes you remember why you live here. The USA match at 11 was calling, so I powered through the morning metrics at the office, then ducked out to The Ballard Tavern with a bunch of coworkers. The place was packed, energy was electric. We didn't play great, but there's something about watching it live with a crowd, burger in hand, that just hits different. Got back to the office feeling restless, spent the afternoon buried in reports. By evening I needed to decompress, so I swung by the bagel spot—got my usual—then lost an hour flipping through vinyl at the record store. Found a reissue I've been hunting for. Rode home through the long twilight feeling oddly content, like the day had rhythm to it.
thought_process: The World Cup buzz definitely pulled me off-task this morning, but I didn't fight it. At 46, I know better than to ignore those spontaneous moments—they're rarer than they used to be. The light rail closure didn't affect me since I bike and bus anyway, but it made me aware of how fortunate my commute is. I was antsy after the match, so the afternoon work felt heavier than usual. I think I needed the evening wander through Ballard to reset. The vinyl find was perfect timing—small win to end on. Sunny Mondays do something to your perspective.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
